methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $3M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Krevera is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Edward Schneeweiss that has raised $3M+. The target operates within a sector represented by 9 private peers (including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics) and 1 public comparable.

Stage-progression analysis applies to this valuation. Using the $3M total raised as a proxy for seed-stage value and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage advancement yields $7.5M. A peer-median approach was not applicable due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables within the robotics and manufacturing sector; while 11 same-sector peers exist, none of the narrower filters produced the three or more datapoints required for reliable calibration.

The triangulated valuation range is $6M to $9M, with a median of $8M. This single-method range reflects narrow confidence intervals around stage-based progression. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the 2.5x multiplier for this specific company's trajectory, the quality and relevance of the broader peer universe, and market conditions affecting seed-to-Series A pricing in frontier robotics. Additional commercial or technical metrics would strengthen conviction in this range.
